title: elaeagnus cumingii subsp. philippensis
intro: Elaeagnus cumingii subsp. philippensis is a shrub or small tree native to the Philippines and other parts of Asia, belonging to the Elaeagnaceae family and characterized by its silvery-gray foliage and showy, fragrant flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Elaeagnus cumingii subsp. philippensis include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and provide relief from digestive problems, as well as its ability to boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made from its leaves, flowers, or roots, and can be used in the form of teas, infusions, or topical applications, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
